name: GALLERY
heroDescription: A responsive digital gallery that gives contemporary art room to lead while making artists, exhibitions and public programming easier to discover. Howell translates the confidence of a physical institution into an accessible cross-device experience.

overview.description: Howell Gallery is a concept platform for exploring contemporary exhibitions beyond a physical visit. It brings together artwork, artist context, current and upcoming shows, activities and editorial material in one coherent catalogue for desktop and mobile.

projectGoal.description: Create an elegant, accessible gallery experience where artwork remains dominant, cultural context is easy to follow and visitors can move naturally from discovery to deeper reading or an in-person activity.

fontAndColours.description: A strict black-and-white foundation gives varied artworks a neutral exhibition wall. Warm amber acts as the institutional signal for navigation, status and important actions, while the Stotzl family keeps titles and long-form cultural content direct.

challenge.description: Cultural websites often split practical information, artwork and scholarly context into separate experiences. Howell needed to support quick exhibition discovery and deep reading without making the interface feel like either a ticketing portal or a dense institutional archive.
challenge.question: How might a digital gallery preserve the presence of the artwork while giving every visitor enough context to feel invited into the conversation?

priorities.title: Let art lead
priorities.description: Use typography and controls as a frame, allowing each exhibition's visual language to remain distinct.

priorities.title: Connect the context
priorities.description: Link works, artists, exhibitions and activities so curiosity never reaches a dead end.

priorities.title: Design for access
priorities.description: Make long-form reading, image detail and practical visit information comfortable on any device.

research.description: Research compared gallery websites, public collection archives and arts publications. The scan revealed a recurring trade-off: visually confident sites often hid practical detail, while information-rich archives treated every visitor like a specialist.

benchmark.note: The comparison identifies interface and content patterns across cultural platforms; it supports a product direction without evaluating curatorial quality.

persona.name: Carmen Vidal
persona.context: Carmen enjoys exhibitions but does not have a formal art background. She discovers shows through social posts, reads on her phone and wants enough context to decide whether an unfamiliar artist is worth exploring in person.
persona.quote: I want the story behind the work, but I do not want to feel that I need an art-history degree before I can start.
goals: See what is on now and what is coming next
Understand an artist and exhibition in approachable language
Save or share an activity for a future visit
frustrations: Artwork presented without scale or context
Biography pages that become walls of text on mobile
Programme details separated from relevant exhibitions

insights.label: 01 · Invitation
insights.title: Context should open a door
insights.description: Plain-language summaries can lead into deeper material without flattening the subject.

insights.label: 02 · Discovery
insights.title: Status matters before filters
insights.description: Current, upcoming and archived states answer the first visitor question faster than a complex taxonomy.

insights.label: 03 · Continuity
insights.title: Every object belongs to a story
insights.description: Works become more meaningful when artist, exhibition and related programming remain one step away.

ideation.description: The concept uses an exhibition as the central narrative object. Visitors can enter through a featured show, an artist, a work or an activity, then move between those contexts without returning to a generic catalogue.

flow.title: Discover
flow.description: Meet a featured exhibition or browse what is currently active.

flow.title: Observe
flow.description: Explore key works with scale, media and focused visual detail.

flow.title: Understand
flow.description: Open an approachable curatorial story and artist profile.

flow.title: Participate
flow.description: Connect talks, workshops or visits to the exhibition context.

flow.title: Return
flow.description: Save, share or continue through related artists and themes.

decisions.label: Core object
decisions.title: Exhibition-led architecture
decisions.description: Shows bring artwork, artists, dates and activities into a narrative visitors already understand.

decisions.label: Catalogue
decisions.title: Status before taxonomy
decisions.description: Active and upcoming labels help casual visitors orient before search and filters add precision.

decisions.label: Reading
decisions.title: Layered interpretation
decisions.description: Short introductions lead to biography, curatorial context and related material at the reader's pace.

decisions.label: Responsive
decisions.title: Editorial reflow
decisions.description: Artwork, captions and text stack deliberately on mobile instead of shrinking a desktop gallery grid.

design.description: The design system behaves like a flexible exhibition frame. Neutral surfaces, confident type and thin rules create order; amber marks institutional wayfinding while artwork introduces the colour, texture and emotion of each page.

decisions.label: Canvas
decisions.title: Neutral by default
decisions.description: White and ink provide consistency across exhibitions with radically different visual identities.

decisions.label: Wayfinding
decisions.title: Amber carries intent
decisions.description: Navigation, status and calls to action share one warm signal without competing with the art.

decisions.label: Typography
decisions.title: Direct, not academic
decisions.description: A sturdy grotesk hierarchy keeps labels, titles and long-form interpretation readable.

decisions.label: Imagery
decisions.title: Space communicates scale
decisions.description: Full-width works alternate with detail crops and contextual installation views.

applications.objective: Help visitors distinguish active, upcoming and archived exhibitions while preserving visual discovery.
applications.solution: Status labels, a featured lead and concise filters establish time and relevance before taxonomy.
applications.outcome: Visitors can orient quickly without losing visual discovery.

applications.objective: Support quick orientation and deeper reading without separating an artist's work from their story.
applications.solution: An identity summary, narrative sections and linked works create multiple reading depths.
applications.outcome: Casual and committed readers share one page comfortably.

applications.objective: Give artwork maximum presence while keeping context and actions accessible on small screens.
applications.solution: The work leads, with caption, metadata and related actions placed in a clear sequence below.
applications.outcome: Small-screen viewing preserves visual presence and context.

testing.description: Prototype review concentrated on comprehension and orientation: finding an active exhibition, explaining its central idea, moving to an artist profile and locating a related activity from both desktop and mobile entry points.
testing.method: Scenario walkthroughs combined first-impression interpretation, catalogue navigation and long-form mobile reading. The resulting observations guided the information hierarchy and responsive behaviour.

findings.observation: Image-led browsing was engaging, but date ranges alone required too much interpretation.
findings.decision: Add plain active and upcoming status labels before exposing deeper filter controls.

findings.observation: The complete biography felt authoritative but difficult to enter on mobile.
findings.decision: Introduce a short profile summary, thematic anchors and links to works before the full narrative.

findings.task: Move from a show to a related activity
findings.observation: A separate programme menu broke the exhibition story.
findings.decision: Surface talks and workshops within the exhibition page while retaining the global activities index.

finalDesign.description: The final experience supports both a quick cultural plan and a deeper encounter. Desktop pages use generous image scale and editorial pacing; mobile views turn the same content into a deliberate sequence of artwork, interpretation and practical action.

highlights.title: Featured exhibition
highlights.description: A focused opening image, statement and date make the current programme immediately legible.

highlights.title: Connected artist profiles
highlights.description: Biography, works and exhibition history form a layered route rather than an isolated archive record.

highlights.title: Accessible catalogue
highlights.description: Status, search and filters balance casual browsing with precise discovery.

highlights.title: Responsive cultural reading
highlights.description: Artwork remains prominent while captions, context and programming reflow into a calm mobile sequence.

impact.description: Howell connects gallery content through one deliberate visitor journey. The design outcome is a flexible cultural publishing model that supports browsing, interpretation and participation without compromising the presence of the art.

metrics.label: Connected catalogue
metrics.description: Exhibitions, artists, artworks and activities link through shared context inside one catalogue.

metrics.label: Layered interpretation
metrics.description: Concise orientation and extended interpretation coexist for casual and specialist visitors.

metrics.label: Consistent discovery
metrics.description: A common system preserves discovery and image presence across desktop and mobile.

learnings.description: Digital access does not mean placing more information beside an artwork. It means designing a sequence in which image, interpretation and practical context each arrive when they can deepen the encounter.

items.title: Neutral interfaces still make choices
items.description: Restraint works only when hierarchy, scale and timing actively support the artwork.

items.title: Accessibility includes cultural confidence
items.description: Plain orientation and layered context help more visitors begin without reducing intellectual depth.

items.title: Responsive design can improve curation
items.description: Mobile sequencing clarified which image, idea and action mattered most at every stage of the story.

gallery.description: Responsive mockups showing the mobile artist profile, desktop featured exhibition, editorial article and searchable exhibition catalogue.
